2604.24062.title: Grounding Before Generalizing: How AI Differs from Humans in Causal Transfer
2604.24062.paper_summary: Extracting abstract causal structures and applying them to novel situations is a hallmark of human intelligence. While Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) have shown strong performance on a wide range of reasoning tasks, their capacity for interactive causal learning -- inducing latent structures through sequential exploration and transferring them across contexts -- remains uncharacterized. Human learners accomplish such transfer after minimal exposure, whereas classical Reinforcement Learning (RL) agents fail catastrophically. Whether state-of-the-art Artificial Intelligence (AI) models possess human-like mechanisms for abstract causal structure transfer is an open question. Using the OpenLock paradigm requiring sequential discovery of Common Cause (CC) and Common Effect (CE) structures, here we show that models exhibit fundamentally delayed or absent transfer: even successful models require initial environmental-specific mapping -- what we term environmental grounding -- before efficiency gains emerge, whereas humans leverage prior structural knowledge from the very first solution attempt. In the text-only condition, models matched or exceeded human discovery efficiency. In contrast, visual information -- in both the image-only and text-and-image conditions -- overall degraded rather than enhanced performance, revealing a broad reliance on symbolic processing rather than integrated multimodal reasoning. Models further exhibited systematic CC/CE asymmetries absent in humans, suggesting heuristic biases rather than direction-neutral causal abstraction. These findings reveal that large-scale statistical learning does not produce the decontextualized causal schemas underpinning human analogical reasoning, establishing grounding-dependent transfer as a fundamental limitation of current LLMs and VLMs.
2604.24062.paper_summary_zh: 提取抽象因果結構並將其應用於新情境是人類智慧的一個特徵。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）和視覺語言模型（VLMs）在各種推理任務上表現出色，但它們在互動式因果學習方面的能力——通過序列探索誘導潛在結構並在不同上下文中轉移它們——仍然未被表徵。人類學習者在最小的暴露後能夠完成這種轉移，而傳統的強化學習（RL）代理則會遭遇災難性的失敗。最先進的人工智慧（AI）模型是否具有人類類似的抽象因果結構轉移機制仍然是一個未解之謎。使用OpenLock範式，要求對共同原因（CC）和共同效果（CE）結構進行序列發現，我們在此顯示模型表現出根本性延遲或缺失的轉移：即使是成功的模型也需要最初的環境特定映射——我們稱之為環境基礎——才能出現效率增益，而人類則從第一次解決嘗試中利用先前的結構知識。在僅有文本的條件下，模型的發現效率與人類相當或超過。相比之下，視覺信息——在僅有圖像和文本與圖像的條件下——整體上降低了而不是提升了性能，顯示出對符號處理的廣泛依賴，而不是綜合的多模態推理。模型還表現出系統性的CC/CE不對稱，而人類則不存在這種情況，這表明存在啟發式偏見，而不是方向中立的因果抽象。這些發現揭示了大規模統計學習並未產生支撐人類類比推理的去情境化因果模式，確立了依賴基礎的轉移作為當前LLMs和VLMs的一個基本限制。
2604.24062.author: Liangru Xiang et.al.
2604.24062.authors: Liangru Xiang, Yuxi Ma, Zhihao Cao, Yixin Zhu, Song-Chun Zhu
